title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mrs. Oliver meets with Poirot to report a murder that occurred the previous night at a Halloween party in Woodleigh, a residential area near London and Manchester. Mrs. Oliver explains that she was visiting a friend, a widow named Judith Butler, and had been invited to provide ideas for the children's party.

The party included traditional games such as biting apples and "Catch the Dragon," which involves burning raisins. Mrs. Oliver reveals that a girl named Joyce, approximately twelve or thirteen years old, was found dead in the library. Joyce had been murdered by someone pushing her head into a heavy, zinc-lined bucket filled with water and floating apples, causing her to drown. Mrs. Oliver notes that the use of a heavy bucket rather than a plastic one might have prevented the incident.

Mrs. Oliver expresses deep distress and a sudden aversion to apples. She clarifies that she did not know Joyce personally but was present because of her connection to Judith Butler. She suggests that the crime may be linked to her own identity, noting that a child at the party recognized her as an author of murder mystery books. Poirot attempts to gather facts regarding the victim's development and the upcoming police interrogation, while Mrs. Oliver implies that the circumstances of the death are more complex than they initially appear.
